slices.focus: Corridor tracking work validated, pattern solidified for handoff
slices.summary: At the annual corridor review, a complete evidence chain validated the mitigation adoption model. Shifted from being a pattern defender to a recognized professional with a documented chain-of-custody approach. Decided to write an internal guide protecting the core elements — chain of custody, designated contacts, and transparent case tracking — while clarifying which parts are transferable and which still require personal participation. Protecting the pattern from being simplified into hollow slogans.
open_loops: Whether the guide draft can be completed before the holiday break or will be pushed into next year
How to redefine personal role — from firefighter putting out individual cases to pattern builder and mentor
How to prevent management from stripping core elements under pressure while still being seen as collaborative
decisions: Write an internal guide centered on chain-of-custody, designated contacts, and transparent case tracking as core elements
Clarify which parts of the model are transferable to others and which still require personal participation
Protect the pattern core from being stripped down to empty slogans under leadership or budget pressure
